17. Training a inverse dynamic models

See seminal paper: Learning Universal Policies via Text-Guided Video Generation

They take a single frame but output a delta with a CNN??

Difficulty with soft robots: Need hand to be rigid otherwise you can’t sense or control it.

17.1. Body Jacobian

Sitzmann paper: Controlling diverse robots byinferring Jacobian fields with deep networks

For any point on the robot, define the body Jacobian as a 3 by n matrix (i.e. how much does varying each of the n joints) cause 3D scene flow.

Task: Take a neural network that maps every 3D point to a 3D Nerf that outputs its Body Jacobian and then use it to solve for the motion when computing 3D scene flow

Turns out you can learn Inverse Dynamics Model Just By Seeing The Robot Execute Random Motions!

Works on soft robots!

17.2. Fine tuning Video Models

  • Can fine tune WAN with a single H200 GPU node. All 14B, tiny compared to LLMs
  • How to make model follows physics? Only answer is to increase data and fine tune on robot data
  • “Large Video Planner Enables Generalizable Robot Control” shows how to do conditioning to make it listen to fisrt frame

[TODO] smart way to train video model autoregressively via diffusion forcing? See rhoda blog

17.3. Frontier

  • Robot Video Models are now viable for product.

  • Two dominant paradigms: video+inverse dynamics, and joint video-action models

    • Inverse dynamics:

      • Rhoda
      • Standard intelligence: with compuetrs

    • Joint video action models: fine tune WAN to output actions

      • Examples: DROID, AGIBOT, DreamZero
      • https://dreamzero0.github.io/

17.4. What’s missing

Cannot do self exploration, e.g. give blackbox and have it figure out how to use it
